claims.text: primary witness: senior US intelligence official, naked-eye observation aided by helicopter spotlight, NVG, and FLIR; multi-hour mission 2025 (date redacted), helicopter call-sign CALL SIGN 1, accompanied by a second senior US intelligence official, two STATE PARTNER ORGANIZATION pilots
location: mountain range west of [SITE CODE NAME], approximately 4 miles east of SITE CODE NAME; helicopter operated from OPERATIONS CENTER
at 2202 hours: LP/OP using FLIR spotted a 'super-hot' orb hovering at ground level, then heading east then south at high speed, then breaking into TWO objects
at 2218 hours: pilots (NVG) and witness (naked eye) spotted a swarm of lights too many to count moving in all directions, generally west of [redacted] heading south

at 2227 hours: two large oval-shaped orange orbs with white/yellow centers appeared in close proximity to CALL SIGN 1, west and above the rotor disc; a third orb flared up below them, then another, until 4-5 orbs were visible in vertical stacking; flared down in reverse order
at 2228 hours: 4-5 similar orbs flared up in horizontal formation above MILITARY AIRCRAFT to the west; flared down sequentially after 10-15 seconds
at 2233 hours: similar orb formation observed to the east toward NEARBY TOWN NAME; horizontal flare-up then flare-down
at 2241 hours: single orb flared up west of SITE CODE NAME over the mountain
at 2249 hours: swarm of lights including three orbs in TRIANGLE FORMATION, west of [redacted]

witness comments: orbs appeared to break off from CALL SIGN 1 and pursue the MILITARY AIRCRAFT; pilots indicated they were recording but many sightings were above the helicopter outside the FLIR camera angle
classification: SECRET//NOFORN (struck through, declassified for release)
